2026-04-22T19:57:06.2779230Z ##[group]Run # Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)
2026-04-22T19:57:06.2779780Z [36;1m# Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)[0m
2026-04-22T19:57:06.2780204Z [36;1m# Mask immediately to prevent timing vulnerabilities[0m
2026-04-22T19:57:06.2780748Z [36;1mAPI_KEY=$(openssl rand -base64 45 | tr -d '/+=')[0m
2026-04-22T19:57:06.2781063Z [36;1mecho "::add-mask::${API_KEY}"[0m
2026-04-22T19:57:06.2781296Z [36;1m[0m
2026-04-22T19:57:06.2781452Z [36;1mPORT=3001[0m
2026-04-22T19:57:06.2781630Z [36;1m[0m
2026-04-22T19:57:06.2781797Z [36;1m# Set outputs for next steps[0m
2026-04-22T19:57:06.2782024Z [36;1m{[0m
2026-04-22T19:57:06.2782225Z [36;1m  echo "safe_outputs_api_key=${API_KEY}"[0m
2026-04-22T19:57:06.2782786Z [36;1m  echo "safe_outputs_port=${PORT}"[0m
2026-04-22T19:57:06.2783052Z [36;1m} >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"[0m

2026-04-22T20:00:05.7395599Z Filed issue **[AgentServer] Docs: Document PlatformHeaders and x-request-id correlation in Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core README** and dispatched triage.
2026-04-22T20:00:05.7397205Z 
2026-04-22T20:00:06.8255315Z **Summary of gaps found in PR #58396:**
2026-04-22T20:00:06.8259404Z - New public `PlatformHeaders` class (with 8 header constants) is not covered in the README Key Concepts section
2026-04-22T20:00:06.8271921Z - The automatic `x-request-id` correlation behavior (OTEL trace ID → incoming header → GUID fallback) is undocumented for end users
2026-04-22T20:00:07.8054649Z - The simplified Tier 3 API (`AddAgentServerCore()` / `UseAgentServerCore()`) replaces 6 calls but isn't clearly explained in the README

2026-04-22T20:00:11.5838951Z ✓ $ grep -n "PlatformHeaders\|RequestIdMiddleware\|RequestIdBaggage\|AddAgentServerCore\|UseAgentServerCore" /home/runner/work/azure-sdk-for-net/azure-sdk-for-net/sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core/README.md
2026-04-22T20:00:11.5840471Z 
2026-04-22T20:00:11.5841479Z ◆ Now I have a clear picture. The README for `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core` has several documentation gaps after the latest commit (PR #58396) which added:
2026-04-22T20:00:11.5842821Z   
2026-04-22T20:00:11.5843915Z   1. `PlatformHeaders` - A public static class with all cross-protocol header name constants. It's mentioned in the CHANGELOG but not in the README's Key Concepts section. As a public API, it should be documented.
2026-04-22T20:00:11.5844713Z   
2026-04-22T20:00:11.5846073Z   2. `RequestIdMiddleware` / `RequestIdBaggagePropagator` - These are internal but the behavior they provide (automatic `x-request-id` header on every response, distributed tracing correlation) is significant for users who need to correlate requests.
2026-04-22T20:00:11.5847384Z      
2026-04-22T20:00:11.5849913Z   3. There's also a breaking change where `IsolationContext.UserIsolationKeyHeaderName` and `IsolationContext.ChatIsolationKeyHeaderName` were removed, and the simplified Tier 3 API now uses just `AddAgentServerCore()` and `UseAgentServerCore()` instead of six separate extension methods. The README touches on this in the Logging section but lacks a dedicated Key Concepts entry or usage example for the new simplified setup.
2026-04-22T20:00:11.5852442Z   
2026-04-22T20:00:11.5858343Z   Looking more carefully at what's missing from the README, I notice `PlatformHeaders` is a public API that users interact with directly, yet it's not documented as a key concept at all. The request ID correlation and tracing behavior gets a brief mention in the Logging section but isn't explained as a foundational concept. Most critically, the breaking change about `IsolationContext` properties being replaced by `PlatformHeaders.UserIsolationKey` and `PlatformHeaders.ChatIsolationKey` isn't reflected anywhere in the README, and the documentation only mentions the `x-platform-server` identity header without explaining the automatic `x-request-id` correlation that happens behind the scenes. This documentation gap needs to be addressed. I'm identifying the core documentation gap: `PlatformHeaders` is a new public API that needs to be documented in the README's Key Concepts section, along with the request ID correlation feature, since downstream u... [truncated: showing first 2000 of 2097 chars]
2026-04-22T20:00:11.5864014Z

2026-04-22T20:00:11.5867557Z 
2026-04-22T20:00:11.5868027Z ◆ The samples don't mention `PlatformHeaders` either. Now I have enough information to file an issue. Let me create the issue now.
2026-04-22T20:00:11.5868744Z   
2026-04-22T20:00:11.5868907Z   Key gaps identified:
2026-04-22T20:00:11.5869633Z   1. `PlatformHeaders` public class not documented in README Key Concepts section - it's a new public API with documented header constants for request ID, server version, session ID, isolation keys, etc.
2026-04-22T20:00:11.5870919Z   2. The `x-request-id` automatic correlation behavior not documented (RequestIdMiddleware is internal but the behavior it provides is notable for developers who need to correlate requests for debugging)
2026-04-22T20:00:11.5872764Z   3. The breaking change: `IsolationContext.UserIsolationKeyHeaderName`/`IsolationContext.ChatIsolationKeyHeaderName` removed and replaced by `PlatformHeaders.UserIsolationKey`/`PlatformHeaders.ChatIsolationKey` - README doesn't reflect this change
2026-04-22T20:00:11.5873794Z   
2026-04-22T20:00:11.5873946Z   
2026-04-22T20:00:11.5874150Z   Now I'm going to file the issue with these findings.

2026-04-22T20:00:11.5877250Z   
2026-04-22T20:00:11.5877442Z   **Summary of gaps found in PR #58396:**
2026-04-22T20:00:11.5877932Z   - New public `PlatformHeaders` class (with 8 header constants) is not covered in the README Key Concepts section
2026-04-22T20:00:11.5878790Z   - The automatic `x-request-id` correlation behavior (OTEL trace ID → incoming header → GUID fallback) is undocumented for end users
2026-04-22T20:00:11.5879655Z   - The simplified Tier 3 API (`AddAgentServerCore()` / `UseAgentServerCore()`) replaces 6 calls but isn't clearly explained in the README

CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 1 issue(s) can be created.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"body":{"description":"Detailed issue description in Markdown. Do NOT repeat the title as a heading since it already appears as the issue's h1. Include context, reproduction steps, or acceptance criteria as appropriate.","type":"string"},"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"labels":{"description":"Labels to categorize the issue (e.g., 'bug', 'enhancement'). Labels must exist in the repository.","items":{"type":"string"},"type":"array"},"parent":{"description":"Parent issue number for creating sub-issues. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 42 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/42). Can also be a temporary_id (e.g., 'aw_abc123', 'aw_Test123') from a previously created issue in the same workflow run.","type":["number","string"]},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"},"temporary_id":{"description":"Unique temporary identifier for referencing this issue before it's created. Format: 'aw_' followed by 3 to 12 alphanumeric characters (e.g., 'aw_abc1', 'aw_Test123'). Use '#aw_ID' in body text to reference other issues by their temporary_id; these are replaced with actual issue numbers after creation.","pattern":"^aw_[A-Za-z0-9]{3,12}$","type":"string"},"title":{"description":"Concise issue title summarizing the bug, feature, or task. The title appears as the main heading, so keep it brief and descriptive.","type":"string"}},"required":["title","body"],"type":"object"},"name":"create_issue"},{"description":"Report that a tool or capability needed to complete the task is not available, or share any information you deem important about missing functionality or limitations. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because the required functionality is missing or access is restricted.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"alternatives":{"description":"Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"reason":{"description":"Explanation of why this tool is needed or what information you want to share about the limitation (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"},"tool":{"description":"Optional: Name or description of the missing tool or capability (max 128 characters). Be specific about what functionality is needed.","type":"string"}},"required":["reason"],"type":"object"},"name":"missing_tool"},{"description":"Log a transparency message when no significant actions are needed. Use this to confirm workflow completion and provide visibility when analysis is complete but no changes or outputs are required (e.g., 'No issues found', 'All checks passed'). This ensures the workflow produces human-visible output even when no other actions are taken.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"message":{"description":"Status or completion message to log. Use this when required tools or data are unavailable and the task cannot be meaningfully performed. This is distinct from noop (no action needed) — it indicates an active failure that prevented the task from running. The workflow framework will treat this as a failure signal even when the agent exits successfully.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"details":{"description":"Optional extended details or diagnostic context about the failure (max 65000 characters).","type":"string"},"reason":{"description":"A concise explanation of why the task could not be completed (max 1024 characters). Use this when required tools or data are unavailable and the task cannot be meaningfully performed. This is distinct from noop (no action needed) — it indicates an active failure that prevented the task from running. The workflow framework will treat this as a failure signal even when the agent exits successfully.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"details":{"description":"Optional extended details or diagnostic context about the failure (max 65000 characters).","type":"string"},"reason":{"description":"A concise explanation of why the task could not be completed (max 1024 characters).
